planName: GRI Flywheel Data Collection and Decision Framework

currentState.revenuePerAd: Spread too thin across 50 ads at $78/day
currentState.metaSignalStrength: WEAK — 50 ads splitting $78/day = $1.56/ad/day. Meta cannot optimise.

phases.name: OBSERVATION ONLY — Do Not Touch Anything
phases.purpose: Let the webhook capture real purchase data. Build the attribution baseline. Do NOT make ad changes based on flywheel data yet.

minimumDataRequired.reasonWhy: Meta needs 7 days of consistent data to show reliable daily patterns. Fewer than 25 orders means any AOV calculation is noise.

actions: Monitor the flywheel log daily — are orders coming through the webhook?
Check UTM attribution rate — what percentage of orders have utm_source=facebook?
Do NOT pause, scale, or change any ads
Do NOT generate a creative brief yet (not enough data to be meaningful)
Log which products are selling and at what AOV manually if needed

decisionGate.canProceedWhen: 25+ orders captured via webhook AND 7+ days of ad snapshots stored
decisionGate.ifNotMet: Extend Phase 1 by 3 days. If UTM attribution is below 30%, check Shopify theme is passing UTM params correctly.

phases.keyMetricToWatch: UTM attribution rate. If orders are coming in but none have utm_source, the join between Meta and Shopify is broken and the whole flywheel is blind.

phases.name: BASELINE WEEK — First Real Numbers
phases.purpose: Establish baseline metrics that all future decisions will be measured against. This is your 'before' snapshot.

minimumDataRequired.reasonWhy: 50 orders gives a statistically useful AOV distribution. 14 days of ad data gives reliable CPA, ROAS, frequency baselines.

actions: Generate the first creative brief on Friday 11 April (2 weeks of data)
Run the AI decision engine for the first time — but REVIEW ONLY, do not approve any actions
Calculate baseline metrics and lock them in: baseline AOV, baseline CPA, baseline ROAS, baseline bundle rate
Identify the top 5 and bottom 5 ads by CPA (not ROAS — CPA is more reliable with low data)
Check frequency on every ad set — anything above 4 on cold is already fatiguing
baselineMetricsToLock: UTM attribution rate (what % of Shopify orders link to a Meta ad)

decisionGate.canProceedWhen: 50+ total orders AND baseline metrics locked in AND at least 3 ads have 10+ purchases each
decisionGate.ifNotMet: Extend Phase 2. If after 14 days you still have fewer than 50 orders, the issue is spend level not data collection time.

phases.name: FIRST KILL CYCLE — Remove the Bleed
phases.purpose: Make the first data backed decisions. Kill underperformers. Consolidate spend into fewer, better ads.

minimumDataRequired.reasonWhy: An ad that has spent 2x your target CPA with zero purchases has had a fair trial. At $78/day across 50 ads, most ads will NEVER get enough spend to prove themselves. This is the core problem.

phases.criticalInsight: You are running 50 ads on $78/day. That is $1.56 per ad per day. Meta CANNOT optimise an ad on $1.56/day. The algorithm needs $20 to $50/day per ad minimum to find the right people. Your first kill cycle is not about bad creatives — it is about consolidating spend into 8 to 10 ads maximum so each one gets $8 to $10/day.
actions: Kill any ad with zero purchases after spending $56+ (2x blended CPA target)
Kill any ad with CPA above $70 (2.5x target) for 3+ days
Kill any ad set with frequency above 5 on cold audiences
Target: reduce from 50 active ads to 10 to 15 maximum
DO NOT increase budget yet — just consolidate
Approve the AI's kill recommendations if they match the data
phases.expectedOutcome: Fewer ads getting more spend each. CPA should drop within 3 to 5 days as Meta's algorithm has more budget per ad to optimise with.

decisionGate.canProceedWhen: Active ads reduced to 15 or fewer AND remaining ads each getting $5+/day AND CPA trending down from baseline
decisionGate.ifNotMet: Kill more aggressively. If CPA is not improving, the issue is creative quality not ad count.

phases.name: AOV PUSH — Bundle Messaging Test
phases.purpose: With clean campaign structure, test bundle-focused creative to push AOV toward $160.

minimumDataRequired.reasonWhy: Testing bundle vs single product messaging needs 15+ purchases per variant to see if AOV difference is real or noise. At 3 to 4 orders/day this takes 2 weeks.

actions: Launch 3 to 4 new bundle focused creatives (from the AI brief)
A/B test: single product ads vs 'Complete Reveal Kit' bundle ads
Track AOV by creative angle — which angles drive $160+ orders?
Set free shipping threshold at $99 (20 to 40% above current $105 AOV is too close — test $89 first)
Test 'backup pack' messaging: 'What if the cannon doesn't fire?'
Monitor bundle rate weekly — target is 30% of orders being multi-item
phases.expectedOutcome: AOV moves from $105 toward $130+. Bundle rate increases from current baseline. Specific creative angles identified that drive higher AOV.

decisionGate.canProceedWhen: AOV above $120 for 7 consecutive days AND bundle rate above 20% AND at least 2 creative angles proven
decisionGate.ifNotMet: Review product page. If people click the bundle ad but buy single items, the issue is the product page not the ad.

phases.name: SCALE — Increase Budget With Confidence
phases.purpose: Scale winning ads and audiences with proven data behind every dollar increase.

minimumDataRequired.reasonWhy: Meta's learning phase requires approximately 50 conversion events per ad set per week. An ad set with 50+ purchases has exited learning and its performance is reliable.

actions: Increase budget 15% on ad sets that meet scale rules (ROAS above 3.0 for 5 days, frequency below 2.5)
Wait 3 days between each budget increase
Move from $78/day toward $120 to $150/day in 15% increments
Launch ASC campaign if not already running (needs $100/day minimum to work properly)
Retargeting should be 15 to 25% of total spend
Approve AI scale recommendations that match the data

budgetScalingSchedule.week2: $90 to $103 (second increase if still on target)

phases.expectedOutcome: Daily spend doubles from $78 to $150 while maintaining CPA within target. ROAS above 3.33 (breakeven). Revenue scales proportionally.

decisionGate.canProceedWhen: Spending $150+/day with blended ROAS above 3.0 AND CPA within target AND AOV above $120
decisionGate.ifNotMet: Scale back to last profitable spend level. Do not chase volume at the expense of profitability.

phases.name: FLYWHEEL MATURITY — System Runs Itself
phases.purpose: The flywheel is now self-sustaining. Every purchase feeds the next creative brief. Every kill rule fires before money is wasted. Josh operates in 15 minutes Mon/Wed/Fri.
systemBehaviour: AI creative brief every Friday uses 60+ days of conversion data — briefs are now highly specific
Kill rules fire automatically with 60 days of baseline data for comparison
Scale rules have enough history to predict outcomes with 70%+ accuracy
AOV Intelligence tracks weekly trends with statistical confidence
Agent learning log has 20+ decision outcomes to calibrate future confidence scores
New creative testing runs in ABO while proven winners scale in CBO/ASC

perAdDecision.why: An ad needs to reach at least 1000 people before you can say its CTR or conversion rate is meaningful. At $10 CPM that costs $10. But CPA decisions need 2x the target spent — if it cannot convert after spending twice what a conversion should cost, it will not convert.

perAdSetDecision.why: Meta's own learning phase requires ~50 events/week but that is aspirational. For GRI at current volume, 10 purchases over 7 days from a single ad set is enough to judge CPA reliability.

perCampaignDecision.why: Campaign level ROAS needs 30+ attributed purchases to be trusted. Below that, a single $200 order can skew the entire ROAS number.

aovDecision.why: AOV is highly variable in gender reveal (one customer buys $40, next buys $200). You need 50+ orders before the average stabilises enough to detect a real shift vs random noise.

creativeBriefQuality.why: A brief written from 5 orders is a guess. A brief written from 20+ orders with 3+ creative angles to compare is a strategy.

immediateProblem.issue: 50 ads on $78/day = $1.56 per ad per day. Meta cannot optimise.
immediateProblem.solution: Phase 3 consolidation is the single most important thing. Cut to 10 to 15 ads. Get each ad $5 to $10/day minimum.
immediateProblem.urgency: Every day you run 50 ads on $78 is money being sprayed. This is why CPA looks high and ROAS looks low — budget is too fragmented for the algorithm to find buyers.
immediateProblem.doNotDo: Do NOT increase budget before consolidating. Adding money to 50 ads just sprays more. Consolidate first, then scale.

flywheelRules.neverDecideFrom1Day: Any metric from a single day is noise. Minimum 3 days for kill rules, 5 days for scale rules, 14 days for strategic decisions.
flywheelRules.neverDecideFromFewOrders: 5 orders is an anecdote. 20 orders is a signal. 50 orders is a pattern. 100 orders is a strategy.
flywheelRules.alwaysCompareToPrevious: Every metric must be compared to its own baseline, not to an arbitrary target. If your CPA was $30 last week and is $25 this week, that is progress even if target is $20.
flywheelRules.respectLearningPhase: After any change (budget, audience, creative), give Meta 3 to 5 days before judging the result. The algorithm is recalibrating.
flywheelRules.failFast: Test 8 to 10 creatives. Kill 80% in week 1. Double down on the 20% that show signal. Repeat.
